wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks the topic — it doesn't fit here because 寿司 is what is liked (the object of preference), not the topic being discussed.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects of transitive verbs — it doesn't fit here because 好き is an adjective expressing preference, not a transitive verb.
wrong_choice_explanations.に: に indicates direction, time, or location — it doesn't fit here because we're expressing what is liked, not direction or location.

wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks known topics — it doesn't fit here because we're asking about an unknown subject (who).
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 誰 is the subject performing the action of coming, not the object.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location of action or means — it doesn't fit here because 誰 is the subject, not a location or method.

wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks topics for contrast or general statements — it doesn't fit here because we're stating that rain is currently happening as immediate information.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 雨 is the subject doing the falling, not the object being acted upon.
wrong_choice_explanations.に: に indicates direction, time, or location — it doesn't fit here because 雨 is the subject of the sentence, not a direction or location.

wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks known topics — it doesn't fit here because we're asking about an unknown subject (what exists).
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects of transitive verbs — it doesn't fit here because 何 is the subject that exists, not an object being acted upon.
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' or 'and' — it doesn't fit here because we're not expressing accompaniment or listing multiple items.

wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks topics for contrast or general statements — it doesn't fit here because we're presenting new information about a specific dog running.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 犬 is the subject performing the action, not the object being acted upon.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location of action or means — it doesn't fit here because 犬 is the subject, not the location where running occurs.

wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks topics — it doesn't fit here because 音楽 is what is being heard (the subject of the passive-like verb), not a topic for comment.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 聞こえる is an intransitive verb expressing natural perception, not a transitive action.
wrong_choice_explanations.から: から means 'from' — it doesn't fit here because 音楽 is what is heard, not the source location it comes from.

wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks topics for general statements — it doesn't fit here because we're reporting a specific event that just happened.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 車 is the subject that stopped, not an object being acted upon.
wrong_choice_explanations.に: に indicates direction, time, or location — it doesn't fit here because 車 is the subject performing the action, not a destination or time.

wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks topics — it doesn't fit here because お茶 is the object of desire with たい, not a topic being discussed.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because with たい (want to), the desired object takes が, not を.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location or means — it doesn't fit here because お茶 is what is wanted, not a location or method.

wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks topics for general statements — it doesn't fit here because we're stating immediate information about the phone ringing now.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 電話 is the subject that is ringing, not an object being acted upon.
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' or 'and' — it doesn't fit here because we're not expressing accompaniment or listing items.

wrong_choice_explanations.は: は marks topics — it doesn't fit here because 宿題 is the subject that got completed, not a topic for general comment.
wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because できる is an intransitive verb meaning 'to be completed/finished', not a transitive action.
wrong_choice_explanations.に: に indicates direction, time, or location — it doesn't fit here because 宿題 is the subject that was completed, not a time or location.
